translations.descricao: Epilepsy is a neurological disorder characterized by recurrent and self-limited seizures, with updated classification by the ILAE considering etiology, comorbidities, and syndrome. Early identification in neonates, infants, and children is essential for management. Cortical development malformations are associated with refractory epilepsy.
translations.sus.indicacao: There is no specific population screening program in the SUS for epilepsy; diagnosis indicated by clinical history of recurrent seizures and neurological evaluation. Recommended in neonates and children with suspicion of epileptic syndromes.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Individuals with suspected convulsive seizures, neonates, infants, and children with risk factors such as cortical malformations.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The ILAE recommends classification and diagnosis of epileptic syndromes based on age of onset, etiology, and EEG for therapeutic guidance. Identification in neonates and childhood for early management.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The global prevalence of epilepsy is approximately 0.5-1% of the population. In neonates and infants, the incidence of epilepsy is high, affecting up to 1-2% in risk groups. In Brazil, prevalence is estimated to be similar to global, around 1%.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The global annual incidence is 40-70 cases per 100,000 inhabitants. In neonates, it can be 1-3 per 1,000 live births.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Mortality associated with epilepsy includes status epilepticus with high morbidity and mortality; SUDEP rate is about 1 per 1,000 patient-years.
